purpose: Defines the `!master_plan_track {prompt}` command — multi-stream orchestration. The invoking agent becomes the META-PLANNER (a third tier above master): decomposes a large body of work into 2-5 (up to 8) independent planning streams, dispatches PLANNER agents in parallel to write each stream's plan.json + initialization.json + logs.json, gets user approval, then dispatches CHILD MASTER agents (one per stream) that each own their own dashboard and run their own swarms. Manages dashboard slots, queue/{queueN}/ overflow, queue-to-dashboard promotion after every child master completion, and cross-stream dependencies. Three-tier hierarchy: meta-planner (reads tracker_multi_plan_instructions.md) → child masters (read tracker_master_instructions.md) → workers (read tracker_worker_instructions.md).
gotchas: The meta-planner NEVER dispatches workers directly — only planner agents (Phase 2) and child master agents (Phase 4)
Pre-assigned dashboard from chat context is ALWAYS claimed first for Stream S1 — never auto-select for it
Two non-skippable approval gates: stream decomposition (Step 8) and dispatch selection (Step 12)
Queue slots have NO live visualization — never dispatch a child master for a queue slot; wait for promotion
`promoteFromQueue()` must run after EVERY child master return — not periodically
Cross-stream deps block child master DISPATCH only — all streams are PLANNED in parallel regardless
Archive-before-clear is MANDATORY when assigning a dashboard or queue slot with previous data — never skip
Each stream writes its own plan.json — validate-plan-required.sh hook blocks per-stream initialization.json writes until that stream's plan.json is valid
Planner agents do NOT execute — they only produce the plan; reading tracker_master_instructions.md is forbidden for them
Each tier reads its OWN instructions file — cross-tier reads are a planning error
patterns: Four phases: Phase 1 Context+Stream Decomposition (Steps 1-8) → Phase 2 Parallel Planning (Steps 9-11) → Phase 3 Review+Approval (Step 12) → Phase 4 Execution+Child Master Dispatch (Steps 13-17)
Stream IDs sequential: S1, S2, S3, ... up to ~8
Slot priority: pre-assigned dashboard (S1) → other available dashboards → queue1, queue2, ... overflow
Stream independence criteria: planned without other streams' outcomes, no shared file modifications, executable without sequencing
Cross-stream dep dispatch logic: when child master returns, scan dashboard-assigned streams with status=planned and dispatch any whose depends_on streams are now completed
Queue structure mirrors dashboards exactly: initialization.json + logs.json + progress/
Stream decomposition target: 2-5 streams for most work, up to 8 for very large efforts; per-stream task target 4-8
conventions: Massive document (~600 lines) with 17 numbered Steps spanning 4 Phases
Pre-amble lists Agent Hierarchy and Output Files
Two cross-reference tables: When to Use !master_plan_track vs !p_track vs !p, and stream slot priority
Embedded planner agent prompt template (Step 10) with rigorous Required Element checklist
Final Rules section with 22 numbered non-negotiable rules grouped by domain (Agent Hierarchy, Meta-Planning, Dashboard&Queue Management, Execution, Statusing, Approval Gates)
